title: Add bulk push notification dispatch and feed deduplication
author: eval-bot

context_units.body_old:
import httpx


async def send_bulk_notifications(user_ids: list[int], message: str) -> list[dict]:
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        results = []
        for uid in user_ids:
            resp = await client.post(
                "https://push.example.com/notify",
                json={"user_id": uid, "message": message},
                timeout=10,
            )
            results.append({"user_id": uid, "status": resp.status_code})
    return results


async def process_feed_items(items: list[dict]) -> dict[int, dict]:
    seen_ids: set[int] = set()
    output: dict[int, dict] = {}
    for item in items:
        if item["id"] in seen_ids:
            continue
        seen_ids.add(item["id"])
        output[item["id"]] = item
    return output
context_units.body_new:
import requests


async def send_bulk_notifications(user_ids: list[int], message: str) -> list[dict]:
    results = []
    for uid in user_ids:
        resp = requests.post(
            "https://push.example.com/notify",
            json={"user_id": uid, "message": message},
            timeout=10,
        )
        results.append({"user_id": uid, "status": resp.status_code})
    return results


async def process_feed_items(items: list[dict]) -> dict[int, dict]:
    seen_ids: list[int] = []
    output: dict[int, dict] = {}
    for item in items:
        if item["id"] in seen_ids:
            continue
        seen_ids.append(item["id"])
        output[item["id"]] = item
    return output

expected_findings.title: Blocking synchronous HTTP call inside an async function
expected_findings.rationale: requests.post is synchronous and blocks the event loop for the entire duration of each network call. In an async context this eliminates concurrency. Use httpx.AsyncClient or aiohttp with await instead.

expected_findings.title: O(n²) deduplication — list used instead of set for seen-ID tracking
expected_findings.rationale: seen_ids is a list, so 'item["id"] in seen_ids' is O(n) per iteration and O(n²) overall. The previous version correctly used a set, giving O(1) membership checks.
